origins: the back alleys of Haven's Folly
a Union naval academy she washed out of
a generation ship that never made port
a Syndicate debt she's still paying
wants: to find a brother who shipped out and never wrote
enough credits to buy back the family farm
to see the edge of the charts with her own eyes
a clean registry and a name nobody's hunting
to prove the academy wrong about washing him out
to bury someone properly, back where they're from
one honest job that doesn't end in gunfire
to stop running long enough to sleep through a night
passage, eventually, to Elysium — if it's real
to matter to somebody who isn't paying her

wounds.text: doesn't trust doctors — will do their own stitches
watched a ship they loved come apart in the black
was left in a cell once and hasn't forgotten it
knew real hunger young and counts the food stores twice
the Union took someone from them and never explained
sold out a crew once, or was sold out — won't say which
the deep black past the charts frightens them, badly
owes money to people who collect in blood

secrets.text: is a Union deserter — a patrol scan is a death sentence
is wanted under another name in three systems
still sends quiet reports to someone dockside
used to fly under a black flag and knows those crews by name
is sicker than they let on and hasn't told a soul
was somebody important once, before the fall

tells.text: goes very quiet when patrols hail
checks the same weld on the bulkhead twice before every fight
touches the hatch frame on the way out, every single time
hums a tune from somewhere you can't place when it's quiet
scans every new dock for a face they never find
stops eating first, quietly, so the others don't notice
